observations.recommendation: We recommend Local Chief Executive to strictly comply with the limitations for the appropriation of discretionary funds provided in Sec. 325 (h) of RA 7160 and refer to Sec. 44 of RA 11518 of the General Appropriations Act of 2021 as guide for its utilization.

1.7 In view of the foregoing, we recommend the strict compliance with the
provisions of Sections 12 and 13 of Republic Act No. 9337, Section 2.57.2
(I) of Revenue Regulations No. 11-2018 dated January 31, 2018 and
Section 4.114 of Revenue Regulations No. 02-98 dated April 17, 1998.

2.5 In relation to this, we recommend the Honorable Mayor to:
a. Fast track the creation of an inventory committee of the
municipality to conduct the physical count of its Road Network
and prepare a report thereon (see Annex C);
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to update the local Road
Network account based on the result of the physical count and
disclose the said account on the Notes to Financial Statements
(see Annex B);
c. Direct the General Services Officer to maintain a Local Road
inventory and Road Map and prepare a report thereon at the
end of every accounting period (See Annex A) as well as keep a
complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and
its components (see Annex E).
d. Require the Municipal Engineer to provide the accountant and the
General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road
components for road projects.

3.5 We recommend Local Chief Executive to direct the municipal
accountant to strictly enforce the submission of Report of Checks
Issued (RCI) and Report of Disbursements (RD) to the auditor within
first ten (10) days of ensuing month, in compliance to Section 7 (7.2.1)
of the Rules and Regulations on Settlement of Accounts (RRSA),
Sections 43 and 44 (k) of the MNGAS for LGU, Volume I.

4.5 We recommend to the Local Chief Executive to direct the municipal
accountant to strictly enforce the submission of report and supporting
documents to the auditor within the first ten (10) days of ensuing month,
in compliance of Section 7 (7.2.1) of the Rules and Regulations on
Settlement of Account (RRSA) and Sections 43 and 44 (k) of the Manual
on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS), for Local
Government Units, Volume I.

5.5 We recommend that the Local Chief Executive shall:
a. Create an inventory committee to conduct the physical count of its
property, plant equipment which shall be made annually and
reported on the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant
and Equipment (RPCPPE), in accordance with Section 156 of COA
Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992; and
b. The reconciliation of the records of the Geneal Service Office and
the Municipal Accountant to up-date the property ledger card to
reflect the true and correct valuation of the said property and
submit the same to the Auditor concerned not later than January
31, of each year, pursuant to Sections 119,120 and 124 of the
Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) by
Local Government Units (LGUs) Volume I.

6.7 We recommend Local Chief Executive to strictly comply with the
limitations for the appropriation of discretionary funds provided in
Sec. 325 (h) of RA 7160 and refer to Sec. 44 of RA 11518 of the General
Appropriations Act of 2021 as guide for its utilization.

7.5 We recommend that the Head of Agency shall instruct the Accountant
to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly
basis, in accordance with COA Circular 92-125-A dated March 4, 1992
and such BRS shall be submitted to the auditor within 15 days from the
receipt of the bank statement, as stated in the same circular, for review
and verification.

8.6 In view of the foregoing, we recommend the Local Chief Executive to
instruct the Accounting and Supply Management Office to coordinate
in the implementation of the Perpetual Inventory method of recording
Inventories. In addition to this, it should be ascertained that necessary
ledger cards are kept by the Accounting Office for each inventory, and
submission of the Report on the Physical Count of Inventory (RPCI) by
the Supply Management Office must be religiously complied with so the
former may make necessary actions or adjustments, if any.

9.7 In line with this, we recommend the Local Chief Executive to:
a) Require the supply/property inspector to conduct pre-repair
inspection and post-repair inspection to the properties subjected
to repairs and furnish the reports to the COA Auditor within
five (5) days from the date of such evaluation/inspection;
b) See to it that a Waste Management Report is prepared when it
involves replacement of worn-out spare parts;
c) Direct the General Services Officer to maintain records of the
history of repairs of each LGU property; and
d) Direct the Municipal Accountant not to process claims
pertaining to repairs and maintenance of motor vehicles and
heavy equipment with lacking supporting documents.
1. That being said, we recommend the management to strictly comply with the provisions of Section 53 of the R.A. No. 9184 in relation to the procedures of Negotiated Procurement.

2. In view of the foregoing, we recommend the management to strictly comply with the provisions of Sections 12 and 13 of Republic Act No. 9337, Section 2.57.2 (I) of Revenue Regulations No. 11-2018 dated January 31, 2018 and Section 4.114 of Revenue Regulations No. 02-98 dated April 17, 1998.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation
That being said, we recommend the management to strictly comply
with the provisions of Section 53 of R.A. No. 9184 in relation to the procedures
of Negotiated Procurement.

Recommendation
In view of the foregoing, we recommend the strict compliance with the
provisions of Sections 12 and 13 of Republic Act No. 9337, Section 2.57.2 (I) of
Revenue Regulations No. 11-2018 dated January 31, 2018 and Section 4.114 of
Revenue Regulations No. 02-98 dated April 17, 1998.

Recommendation
Thus, we recommend that the Local Chief Executive to direct the
Municipal Accountant/Treasurer to strictly comply with the provisions of the
above stated circulars to maintain a sound internal control in the disbursement
of government funds.

Recommendation
In relation to this, we recommend the Honorable Mayor to:
a. Abide Section V.1, V.2, V.3, V.8, V.9 of the Commission on Audit Circular
2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.
b. Create an inventory committee of the municipality to conduct the physical
count of its Road Network and prepare a report thereon (see Annex C);
c. Require the Municipal Accountant to update the local Road Network
account based on the result of the physical count and disclose the said
account on the Notes to Financial Statements (see Annex B);
d. Direct the General Services Officer to maintain a Local Road inventory
and Road Map and prepare a report thereon at the end of every accounting
period (See Annex A) as well as keep a complete Local Road Network
Property Card for all roads and its components (see Annex E); and
e. Require the Municipal Engineer to provide the accountant and the General
Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road
components for road projects.

Recommendation
We recommend Local Chief Executive to direct the municipal
accountant to strictly enforce the submission of Report of Checks Issued (RCI)
and Report of Disbursements (RD) to the auditor within first ten (10) days of
ensuing month, in compliance to Section 7 (7.2.1) of the Rules and Regulations
on Sections 43 and 44 (k) of the MNGAS for LGUS, Volume I. Settlement of
Account prescribed under Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-
006 dated September 15, 2009 and LGU NGAS Vol. I.

Recommendation
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to require:
a. The General Services Officer to create an inventory committee to conduct
the physical count of its properties, plant equipment which shall be made
annually and reported on the Report on the Physical Count of Property,
Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE), in accordance with Section 156 of COA
Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992.
b. The reconciliation of the record of the GSO and the Municipal Accountant
to up-date the property ledger card to reflect the true and correct valuation
of the said property and submit the same to the Auditor concerned not
later than January 31, of each year, pursuant to Furthermore, Section
119,120 and 124 of the Manual New Government Accounting System
(MNGAS) by Local Government Unit Volume I.

Recommendation
Taking this to consideration, we recommend the Honorable Mayor to
direct the Municipal Accountant to compute and record the depreciation of
Property Plant Equipment. Compute the depreciation using the straight-line
method and depreciate it using the table of estimated useful life provided
under COA Circular 2017-004.

Recommendation
We recommend Local Chief Executive to direct the municipal
accountant to strictly enforce the submission of report and supporting
documents to the auditor within first ten (10) days of ensuing month in
compliance of Section 7 (7.2.1) of the Rules and Regulations on Settlement of
Account RRSA and LGU NGAS Vol. I.
We recommend that Head of Agency to strictly enforce the compliance of Section 7 (7.2.1) of the Rules and Regulations on Settlement of Account RRSA Section 179 of GAAM.

observations.recommendation:
In relation to this, we recommend the Honorable Mayor:
a. Abide Section V.1, V.2, V.3, V.8 and V.9 of Commission On Audit
Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.
b. Create an inventory committee of the municipality to conduct the
physical count of its Road Network and prepare a report thereon (see
Annex C);
c. Require the Municipal Accountant to update the local Road Network
account based on the result of the physical count and disclose the said
account on the Notes to Financial Statements (see Annex B);
d. Direct the General Services Officer to maintain a Local Road inventory
and Road Map and prepare a report thereon at the end of every
15
e. accounting period (See Annex A) as well as keep a complete Local Road
Network Property Card for all roads and its components (see Annex E).
f. Require the Municipal Engineer to provide the accountant and the
General Services Officer with the complete description and cost
segregation of road components for road projects.

Taking this to consideration, we recommend the Honorable Mayor to direct
the Municipal Accountant to compute and record the depreciation of Property Plant
Equipment in accordance with Section 59 Philippine Public Sector Accounting
Standards (PPSAS 17)- Property Plant and Equipment (PPE). Compute the
depreciation using the straight line method and depreciate it using the table of
estimated useful life provided under Philippine Application Guidance (PAG) 4, 5
and 6 of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards 17 (PPSAS 17)-
Property Plant and Equipment (PPE).

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive requires:
b. The General Services Officer to create an inventory committee to
conduct the physical count of its properties, plant equipment, and
shall be made annually and reported on the Report on the Physical
Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE).
c. The reconciliation of the record of the GSO and the Municipal
Accountant to up-date the property ledger card to reflect the true and
correct valuation of the said property and submit the same to the
Auditor concerned not later than January 31, of each year.

We recommend that the Head of Agency instruct the Accountant to prepare
the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance with
Section 74 of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as the
Government Auditing Code of the Philippines and Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of the
Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 and such
BRS shall be submitted to the auditor within 10 days from the receipt of the bank
statement for review and verification.

We recommend that the Head of Agency shall instruct the Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance with COA Circular 92-125-A dated March 4, 1992.  Such BRS shall be regularly submitted to the auditor for review and verification.

The reconciliation of the record of the GSO and the Municipal Accountant to up-date the property ledger card to reflect the true and correct valuation of the said property and submit the same to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31, of each year.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Head of Agency shall instruct the Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance with COA Circular 92-125-A dated March 4, 1992.  Such BRS shall be regularly submitted to the auditor for review and verification.

We recommend the Treasurer and Accountant to prepare and submit the monthly reports, quarterly reports and final reports/statements and other required reports within the time frame prescribed under the existing policies and to see to it that these reports are timely submitted to the Office of the Auditor. Further, to submit the Disbursement Vouchers of P1,293,049.69 which was funded by ORT-ARMM
